description_long: This object is a preserved crab, featuring a broad, rounded carapace with a rough, mottled brown surface. The crab has two prominent claws (chelae) at the front, each robust and slightly curved, with visible segmentation and jointed articulation. Its body is flanked by four pairs of slender, jointed walking legs on each side, tapering to pointed tips. The underside is lighter in color, with a pale, almost white ventral surface and visible segmentation. The crab's eyes are not clearly visible, and no antennae are apparent in the images. The overall appearance suggests a natural, dried specimen, with a hard exoskeleton and a slightly matte, textured finish.
description: A preserved brown crab with a broad, rough carapace, two large claws, and eight slender, jointed legs. The underside is pale and segmented, with a hard, matte shell.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broad carapace, both large claws, and the symmetrical arrangement of the walking legs.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the crab's body depth, the curvature of the claws, and the layered segmentation of the legs.
description_view_2: Rear (ventral) view displaying the pale, segmented underside and the arrangement of the legs and claws from below.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the crab's body thickness and the articulation of the legs and claws.
